from source import RedBlackTree

def test_floor_method():
    """Test the floor method of RedBlackTree with different cases"""
    # Create a tree with several values
    tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
    tree.insert(5)
    tree.insert(15)
    tree.insert(3)
    tree.insert(7)
    tree.insert(12)
    tree.insert(20)
    
    # Test cases:
    
    # Case 1: Exact match
    assert tree.floor(10) == 10
    assert tree.floor(5) == 5
    assert tree.floor(20) == 20
    
    # Case 2: Value is less than minimum - should return None
    assert tree.floor(1) is None
    
    # Case 3: Value is between existing values - should return the largest value less than or equal to it
    assert tree.floor(6) == 5
    assert tree.floor(11) == 10
    assert tree.floor(13) == 12
    assert tree.floor(18) == 15
    
    # Case 4: Value is greater than maximum - should return the maximum
    assert tree.floor(25) == 20
    
    print("All Python floor tests passed!")
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_floor_method()

        """Test that property 4 violation is detected (red nodes must have black children)."""
        # Create a tree where a red node has a red child
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)  # Black root
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 1, tree, None, None)  # Red node
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 1, tree.left, None, None)  # Red child of red node
        
        # This should fail with "Property 4" message
        self.assertFalse(tree.check_color_properties())
    
    def test_property5_violation(self):
        """Test that property 5 violation is detected (all paths must have same black height)."""
        # Create a tree with inconsistent black heights

        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black node

        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(12, 1, tree.right, None, None)  # Red node

        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black node
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black node
        
        # Mock methods to return values that pass the checks
        original_check_coloring = tree.check_coloring
        tree.check_coloring = lambda: True
        
        original_black_height = tree.black_height
        tree.black_height = lambda: 2  # Non-None value

        # Restore the original methods

        tree.black_height = original_black_height

import unittest
import sys
import os
sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestPostorderTraverse(unittest.TestCase):
    
    def test_empty_tree(self):
        tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [None])
    
    def test_single_node(self):
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [5])
    
    def test_left_child_only(self):
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(3, 1, tree, None, None)
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [3, 5])
    
    def test_right_child_only(self):
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(7, 1, tree, None, None)
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [7, 5])
    
    def test_both_children(self):
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(3, 1, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(7, 1, tree, None, None)
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [3, 7, 5])
    
    def test_complex_tree(self):
        # Create a more complex tree
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(3, 1, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(8, 1, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(1, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(4, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(9, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        result = list(tree.postorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [1, 4, 3, 7, 9, 8, 5])

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)
        tree.insert(12)
        tree.insert(20)

        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(5), 5)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(15), 15)
        
        # Test ceil values
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(4), 5)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(6), 7)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(11), 12)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(0), 3)
        
        # Test edge cases
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(20), 20)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(25), None)  # No ceiling for values greater than max

    def test_ceil_null_label(self):

        tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.label = 10
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)

        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(7), 10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(12), 15)

        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = None
        tree.right = None
        
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(5), 10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(10), 10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.ceil(15), None)

        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)

import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestSearchMethod(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        # Create a basic tree for testing
        self.tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        self.tree.insert(5)
        self.tree.insert(15)
        self.tree.insert(3)
        self.tree.insert(7)
        self.tree.insert(12)
        self.tree.insert(20)
        
    def test_search_root_node(self):
        """Test searching for the root node"""
        result = self.tree.search(10)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 10)
        
    def test_search_leaf_node(self):
        """Test searching for leaf nodes"""
        result = self.tree.search(3)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 3)
        
        result = self.tree.search(7)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 7)
        
        result = self.tree.search(12)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 12)
        
        result = self.tree.search(20)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 20)
        
    def test_search_middle_node(self):
        """Test searching for middle nodes"""
        result = self.tree.search(5)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 5)
        
        result = self.tree.search(15)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 15)
        
    def test_search_nonexistent_node(self):
        """Test searching for nodes that don't exist"""
        # Less than any node in tree
        result = self.tree.search(1)
        self.assertIsNone(result)
        
        # Between existing nodes
        result = self.tree.search(6)
        self.assertIsNone(result)
        
        # Greater than any node in tree
        result = self.tree.search(25)
        self.assertIsNone(result)
        
    def test_search_with_none_label(self):
        """Test searching in a tree with None label"""
        empty_tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        result = empty_tree.search(10)
        self.assertIsNone(result)
        
    def test_search_complex_tree(self):
        """Test search in a more complex tree"""
        complex_tree = RedBlackTree(50, 0, None, None, None)
        complex_tree.insert(25)
        complex_tree.insert(75)
        complex_tree.insert(10)
        complex_tree.insert(30)
        complex_tree.insert(60)
        complex_tree.insert(90)
        complex_tree.insert(5)
        complex_tree.insert(15)
        
        # Test searching for various nodes
        self.assertEqual(complex_tree.search(50).label, 50)
        self.assertEqual(complex_tree.search(25).label, 25)
        self.assertEqual(complex_tree.search(5).label, 5)
        self.assertEqual(complex_tree.search(90).label, 90)
        self.assertIsNone(complex_tree.search(100))
        self.assertIsNone(complex_tree.search(55))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

from source import RedBlackTree

class TestRotateRight(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_basic_rotate_right(self):
        """Test a basic right rotation scenario"""
        # Create initial tree
        #      A
        #     / \
        #    B   C
        #   / \
        #  D   E
        # 
        # After rotation:
        #     B
        #    / \
        #   D   A
        #      / \
        #     E   C
        
        tree = RedBlackTree("A", 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree("B", 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree("C", 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree("D", 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree("E", 0, tree.left, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation
        new_root = tree.rotate_right()
        
        # Assertions
        self.assertEqual(new_root.label, "B")
        self.assertEqual(new_root.left.label, "D")
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.label, "A")
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.left.label, "E")
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.right.label, "C")
        
        # Check parent pointers
        self.assertIsNone(new_root.parent)
        self.assertEqual(new_root.left.parent, new_root)
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.parent, new_root)
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.left.parent, new_root.right)
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.right.parent, new_root.right)

    def test_rotate_right_no_left_child(self):
        """Test that rotate_right returns the original node when there's no left child"""
        tree = RedBlackTree("A", 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree("C", 0, tree, None, None)
        
        # No left child, so should return original tree
        result = tree.rotate_right()
        self.assertEqual(result, tree)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, "A")

    def test_rotate_right_with_null_right_of_left(self):
        """Test rotation when the left child has no right child"""

        tree.left = RedBlackTree("B", 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree("C", 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree("D", 0, tree.left, None, None)
        
        new_root = tree.rotate_right()

        self.assertEqual(new_root.left.label, "D")
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.label, "A")
        self.assertIsNone(new_root.right.left)
        self.assertEqual(new_root.right.right.label, "C")

    console.assert(newRoot.right.left.parent === newRoot.right, "E's parent should be A");
    console.assert(newRoot.right.right.parent === newRoot.right, "C's parent should be A");

class TestInsertRepair(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_root_node_case(self):
        """Test when the node is the root and should be colored black."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(0, 1, None, None, None)  # Create with red color (1)

        """Test when the parent is black and the node should be colored red."""
        parent = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)  # Black parent
        child = RedBlackTree(5, 0, parent, None, None)  # Child with black color initially
        parent.left = child
        child._insert_repair()
        self.assertEqual(child.color, 1, "Child with black parent should be colored red (1)")

        uncle = RedBlackTree(10, 0, grandparent, None, None)  # Black uncle
        child = RedBlackTree(25, 1, parent, None, None)  # Red child
        
        # Connect nodes
        grandparent.right = parent
        grandparent.left = uncle
        parent.left = child
        
        # Test the repair function

        # Verify right rotation occurred

        self.assertTrue(hasattr(child.right, '_insert_repair'), "Child's right should have _insert_repair method")

        # Connect nodes
        grandparent.left = parent
        grandparent.right = uncle
        parent.right = child
        
        # Test the repair function

        # Verify left rotation occurred
        self.assertTrue(child.left is not None, "Child should have left child after rotation")
        self.assertTrue(hasattr(child.left, '_insert_repair'), "Child's left should have _insert_repair method")
    
    def test_black_uncle_left_child_same_side_case(self):
        """Test case with black uncle, node is left child, parent is left child."""

        right_sibling = RedBlackTree(15, 1, parent, None, None)  # Red sibling
        
        # Connect nodes

        grandparent.right = uncle
        parent.left = child
        parent.right = right_sibling
        
        # Test the repair function

        # Verify rotation and color changes

        self.assertEqual(get_color(parent.right), 1, "Parent's right child should be red after repair")
    
    def test_black_uncle_right_child_same_side_case(self):

        # Verify rotation and color changes

        self.assertEqual(get_color(parent.left), 1, "Parent's left child should be red after repair")
    
    def test_red_uncle_case(self):

        grandparent = RedBlackTree(20, 0, None, None, None)  # Black grandparent initially

        # Parent should have called _remove_repair

import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree, get_color

class TestRemoveRepair(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_remove_repair_sibling_red(self):
        """Test _remove_repair when sibling is red."""
        # Create a tree structure where sibling is red
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)  # Node being repaired
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 1, tree, None, None)  # Red sibling
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(25, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        # Execute the repair
        tree.left._remove_repair()
        
        # After repair, sibling should be black, parent red
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree.right), 0)
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree), 1)
        
        # Tree should be rotated
        self.assertEqual(tree.parent, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.label, 20)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.left.label, 5)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.right.label, 15)
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.label, 25)
    
    def test_remove_repair_sibling_black_parent_black_children_black(self):
        """Test _remove_repair when sibling is black, parent is black, and sibling's children are black."""
        # Create a tree structure
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)  # Black parent
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)  # Node being repaired
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black sibling
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)  # Black child
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(25, 0, tree.right, None, None)  # Black child
        # Add a grandparent for recursive repair
        parent_tree = RedBlackTree(0, 0, None, None, None)
        parent_tree.right = tree
        tree.parent = parent_tree
        
        # Execute the repair
        tree.left._remove_repair()
        
        # After repair, sibling should be red
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree.right), 1)
        
        # Parent should have called _remove_repair
        # We can't check if it was called, but we can check the tree is still valid
        self.assertEqual(tree.parent, parent_tree)
    
    def test_remove_repair_sibling_black_parent_red_children_black(self):
        """Test _remove_repair when sibling is black, parent is red, and sibling's children are black."""
        # Create a tree structure
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 1, None, None, None)  # Red parent
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)  # Node being repaired
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black sibling
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)  # Black child
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(25, 0, tree.right, None, None)  # Black child
        
        # Execute the repair
        tree.left._remove_repair()
        
        # After repair, sibling should be red, parent black
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree.right), 1)
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree), 0)
    
    def test_remove_repair_left_child_case(self):
        """Test _remove_repair when node is left child, sibling is black, 
        sibling's right child is black and left child is red."""
        # Create a tree structure
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)  # Black parent
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)  # Node being repaired (left child)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)  # Black sibling
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 1, tree.right, None, None)  # Red left child
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(25, 0, tree.right, None, None)  # Black right child
        
        # Execute the repair
        tree.left._remove_repair()
        
        # After repair, sibling should be black and right child of sibling should be red
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.label, 15)  # Sibling is now 15 after rotation
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree.right), 0)  # Sibling is black
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.right.label, 20)  # Right child of new sibling is 20
        self.assertEqual(get_color(tree.right.right), 1)  # Right child of new sibling is red
    
    def test_remove_repair_right_child_case(self):
        """Test _remove_repair when node is right child, sibling is black, 
        sibling's right child is red and left child is black."""

import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestInsertEquivalence(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_empty_tree_insert(self):
        """Test inserting into an empty tree"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        tree = tree.insert(10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(tree.color, 0)  # Should be black (0)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.left)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.right)

    def test_duplicate_insert(self):
        """Test inserting a duplicate value"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        result = tree.insert(10)
        self.assertEqual(result.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(result, tree)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.left)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.right)

    def test_insert_left(self):
        """Test inserting a value to the left"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        result = tree.insert(5)
        self.assertEqual(result, tree)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.label, 5)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.color, 1)  # Should be red (1)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.parent, tree)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.right)

    def test_insert_right(self):
        """Test inserting a value to the right"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        result = tree.insert(15)
        self.assertEqual(result, tree)
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.label, 15)
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.color, 1)  # Should be red (1)
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.parent, tree)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.left)

    def test_recursive_insert(self):
        """Test recursive insertion into existing subtree"""
        # For recursive insertion, we'll test that the values are in the tree
        # Rather than testing the exact structure, which may change due to balancing
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree = tree.insert(5)
        tree = tree.insert(3)
        
        # Check that all values are in the tree
        # We know the insert implementation works because the existing tests pass
        self.assertIn(10, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        self.assertIn(5, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        self.assertIn(3, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        
        # Check if inorder traversal gives correct sorted order
        inorder_vals = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(sorted(inorder_vals), inorder_vals)
        
        # Test insertion to the right
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree = tree.insert(15)
        tree = tree.insert(17)
        
        # Check that all values are in the tree
        self.assertIn(10, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        self.assertIn(15, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        self.assertIn(17, list(tree.inorder_traverse()))
        
        # Check if inorder traversal gives correct sorted order
        inorder_vals = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(sorted(inorder_vals), inorder_vals)

    def test_return_value(self):
        """Test that the method returns the root node"""
        # Create a tree with a parent
        root = RedBlackTree(20, 0, None, None, None)
        left = RedBlackTree(10, 1, root, None, None)
        root.left = left
        
        # Insert into the left subtree
        result = left.insert(5)
        # In Python, it should return the parent or self
        self.assertTrue(result == root or result == left)  
        
        # Create a tree without a parent (root node)
        root = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        result = root.insert(5)
        self.assertEqual(result, root)  # Should return self if no parent

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

parsed_final_response.source_test_file_implementation:
import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestInorderTraverse(unittest.TestCase):
    
    def test_empty_tree(self):
        """Test inorder traversal on an empty tree (only root with None label)."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        result = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [None])
    
    def test_single_node(self):
        """Test inorder traversal on a tree with just the root node."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        result = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [5])
    
    def test_balanced_tree(self):
        """Test inorder traversal on a balanced tree."""
        # Create a simple balanced tree with 7 nodes
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(12, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(17, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        result = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [3, 5, 7, 10, 12, 15, 17])
    
    def test_unbalanced_tree(self):
        """Test inorder traversal on an unbalanced tree."""
        # Create an unbalanced tree (right-heavy)
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right.right = RedBlackTree(25, 0, tree.right.right, None, None)
        
        result = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        self.assertEqual(result, [10, 15, 20, 25])
    
    def test_complex_tree(self):
        """Test inorder traversal on a more complex tree."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(8, 0, None, None, None)
        # Insert some values (using the insert method to ensure proper RBT structure)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(10)
        tree.insert(1)
        tree.insert(6)
        tree.insert(14)
        tree.insert(4)
        tree.insert(7)
        tree.insert(13)
        
        # The traversal should return elements in ascending order
        result = list(tree.inorder_traverse())
        expected = [1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 10, 13, 14]
        self.assertEqual(result, expected)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

    get_color = require('./translated').get_color;
} catch (e) {
    // Handle case where RedBlackTree is not exported properly
    const exported = require('./translated');
    RedBlackTree = exported.RedBlackTree || exported;
    get_color = exported.get_color || function(node) {

            return 0;
        } else {
            return node.color;
        }
    };

        console.log("Test get_max empty tree: " + (result ? "PASSED" : "FAILED"));

    }

        const tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, null, null, null);
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(10, 0, tree, null, null);
        const result = tree.get_max() === 10;
        console.log("Test get_max with right child: " + (result ? "PASSED" : "FAILED"));
        return result;
    }
    
    // Test 3: Test get_max method with multiple right children
    function test_get_max_deep_right_path() {
        const tree = RedBlackTree(5, 0, null, null, null);
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(10, 0, tree, null, null);
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, null, null);
        tree.right.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right.right, null, null);
        const result = tree.get_max() === 20;
        console.log("Test get_max deep right path: " + (result ? "PASSED" : "FAILED"));
        return result;
    }

        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, null, null);
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, null, null);

        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, null, null);
        const result = tree.get_max() === 20;
        console.log("Test get_max with mixed children: " + (result ? "PASSED" : "FAILED"));
        return result;

        test_get_max_with_right_child(),

import unittest
import re
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestContainsMethod(unittest.TestCase):
    """Test the __contains__ method of RedBlackTree."""
    
    def test_contains_with_match(self):
        """Test when the search method finds a match."""
        # Create a mock tree with a search method that returns an object
        tree = RedBlackTree(0, 0, None, None, None)
        
        # Override the search method to always return a mock object (not None)
        def mock_search_with_match(label):
            return RedBlackTree(label, 0, None, None, None)
        
        tree.search = mock_search_with_match
        
        # Test that __contains__ returns True when search returns an object
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(10))
        
    def test_contains_without_match(self):
        """Test when the search method does not find a match."""
        # Create a mock tree with a search method that returns None
        tree = RedBlackTree(0, 0, None, None, None)

parsed_final_response.source_test_file_implementation:
import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestGetMin(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_empty_tree(self):
        """Test get_min on an empty tree (only root node)"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(42, 0, None, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.get_min(), 42)
    
    def test_only_right_children(self):
        """Test get_min on a tree with only right children"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(30, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.get_min(), 10)
    
    def test_with_left_children(self):
        """Test get_min on a tree with left children"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.get_min(), 3)
    
    def test_complex_tree(self):
        """Test get_min on a complex tree"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.left.left = RedBlackTree(1, 0, tree.left.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(12, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.get_min(), 1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

  "source_test_file_implementation": "import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestSiblingMethod(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_sibling_null_parent(self):
        # Test case where parent is None
        tree = RedBlackTree(0, 0, None, None, None)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.sibling())
        
    def test_sibling_left_child(self):
        # Test case where node is left child of parent
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.left.sibling(), tree.right)
        
    def test_sibling_right_child(self):
        # Test case where node is right child of parent
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.right.sibling(), tree.left)
        
    def test_sibling_one_child_only(self):
        # Test case where parent has only one child
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.left.sibling())
        
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertIsNone(tree.right.sibling())
        
    def test_complex_tree(self):
        # Test in a more complex tree structure
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(12, 0, tree.right, None, None)

1. If the node to be removed has two children, it finds the maximum value in the left subtree and replaces the current node's label with that value, then removes that node.
2. If the node has at most one child and is red, it simply removes itself from the parent.
3. If the node is black with no children, it performs removal repair operations before unlinking itself.
4. If the node is black with one red child, it replaces its data with the child's and adjusts pointers.
5. If the label to remove is less than the current node's label, it searches in the left subtree.
6. If the label to remove is greater than the current node's label, it searches in the right subtree.
7. Both implementations return the parent of the removed node or the node itself if there's no parent.

The JavaScript code correctly translates all conditional branches, variable assignments, and recursive calls from the Python implementation while maintaining the same Red-Black Tree invariants and balancing logic.",
  "formal_spec": "function remove(label):
  - Pre-condition: The tree is a valid Red-Black Tree, label is a comparable value
  - Post-condition: Returns the modified tree with the node containing label removed while maintaining Red-Black Tree properties
  - Behavior:
    1. If node with label is found:
      a. If node has two children:
         - Replace node's label with maximum value from left subtree
         - Remove that maximum value node from left subtree
      b. If node has at most one child:
         - If node is red: Simply unlink from parent
         - If node is black with no children:
           * If node has no parent: Return empty tree
           * Otherwise: Repair tree to maintain RB properties, then unlink from parent
         - If node is black with one red child: Replace node's data with child's data
    2. If label is less than current node's label and left child exists:
       - Recursively remove from left subtree
    3. If label is greater than current node's label and right child exists:
       - Recursively remove from right subtree
    4. Return parent of removed node or self if no parent exists",
  "counter_example": ""
}
</final_response_format>

        tree = tree.remove(10)
        
        # After removal, the root should be replaced with the maximum value from left subtree (7)
        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(10))

        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(7))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(12))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(15))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(20))
        
    def test_remove_black_node_with_no_children(self):
        """Test removing a black node with no children (requires rebalancing)"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)
        tree.insert(12)
        tree.insert(20)
        
        # Make sure the tree is valid before removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.check_color_properties())
        
        # Remove a leaf node that's black (may require rebalancing)

            node = tree.search(node_val)
            if node and node.color == 0 and not node.left and not node.right:
                tree = tree.remove(node_val)
                self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(node_val))
                break
                
        # Verify the tree is still valid after removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.check_color_properties())
        
    def test_remove_red_node(self):
        """Test removing a red node"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)

        for node_val in [3, 5, 7, 15]:
            node = tree.search(node_val)
            if node and node.color == 1:
                tree = tree.remove(node_val)
                self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(node_val))
                break
                
        # Verify the tree is still valid after removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.check_color_properties())
        
    def test_remove_nonexistent_node(self):
        """Test removing a node that doesn't exist in the tree"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        
        # Before removal attempt

        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(5))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(10))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(15))
        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(100))
        
    def test_remove_root_in_single_node_tree(self):

import sys
import os

    def test_remove_leaf_node(self):

        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)

        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(3))

        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(7))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(10))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(15))
        
    def test_remove_node_with_one_child(self):
        """Test removing a node with one child"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        
        # Before removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(5))
        
        # Remove node with one child
        tree = tree.remove(5)
        
        # After removal
        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(5))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(3))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(10))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(15))
        
    def test_remove_node_with_two_children(self):
        """Test removing a node with two children"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)
        tree.insert(12)
        tree.insert(20)
        
        # Before removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(10))
        
        # Remove root node with two children
        tree = tree.remove(10)
        
        # After removal, the root should be replaced with the maximum value from left subtree (7)
        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(10))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(3))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(5))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(7))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(12))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(15))
        self.assertTrue(tree.__contains__(20))
        
    def test_remove_black_node_with_no_children(self):
        """Test removing a black node with no children (requires rebalancing)"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)
        tree.insert(12)
        tree.insert(20)
        
        # Make sure the tree is valid before removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.check_color_properties())
        
        # Remove a leaf node that's black (may require rebalancing)
        # First, find a black leaf node
        for node_val in [3, 7, 12, 20]:
            node = tree.search(node_val)
            if node and node.color == 0 and not node.left and not node.right:
                tree = tree.remove(node_val)
                self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(node_val))
                break
                
        # Verify the tree is still valid after removal
        self.assertTrue(tree.check_color_properties())
        
    def test_remove_red_node(self):
        """Test removing a red node"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.insert(5)
        tree.insert(15)
        tree.insert(3)
        tree.insert(7)

        self.assertFalse(tree.__contains__(100))
        
    def test_remove_root_in_single_node_tree(self):
        """Test removing the root in a tree with only one node"""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        
        # Remove the only node
        new_tree = tree.remove(10)
        
        # The result should be a new empty tree
        # The RedBlackTree() constructor returns a new empty tree when passed None

    tree.insert(15);
    tree.insert(3);
    tree.insert(7);
    tree.insert(12);
    tree.insert(20);

    assert(tree.check_color_properties(), "Tree should be valid before removal");

    // Try to remove a nonexistent node
    const result = tree.remove(100);
    
    // After removal attempt, the tree should remain unchanged
    assert(result.__contains__(5), "Tree should still contain 5 after removal attempt");
    assert(result.__contains__(10), "Tree should still contain 10 after removal attempt");
    assert(result.__contains__(15), "Tree should still contain 15 after removal attempt");
    assert(!result.__contains__(100), "Tree should not contain 100 after removal attempt");
    
    console.log("testRemoveNonexistentNode: PASSED");
    return true;
}

function testRemoveRootInSingleNodeTree() {
    // Test removing the root in a tree with only one node
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, null, null, null);
    
    // Remove the only node
    const newTree = tree.remove(10);
    
    // The result should be an empty tree (with label=null)
    assert(newTree.label === null, "Tree should be empty after removing root");
    
    console.log("testRemoveRootInSingleNodeTree: PASSED");
    return true;
}

// Run all tests
function runTests() {
    let passed = 0;
    const tests = [
        testRemoveLeafNode,
        testRemoveNodeWithOneChild,
        testRemoveNodeWithTwoChildren,
        testRemoveBlackNodeWithNoChildren,
        testRemoveRedNode,
        testRemoveNonexistentNode,
        testRemoveRootInSingleNodeTree
    ];
    
    for (let i = 0; i < tests.length; i++) {
        try {
            if (tests[i]()) {
                passed++;
            }
        } catch (e) {
            console.error(`Test ${tests[i].name} failed: ${e.message}`);
        }
    }
    
    console.log(`${passed}/${tests.length} tests passed`);
}

// Add module exports for the tests
module.exports = { runTests };

// Run tests when this file is executed directly
if (require.main === module) {
    runTests();
}",

  "is_equivalent": "no",
  "explanation": "While the overall control flow structure is similar between the Python and JavaScript versions, there's a significant difference in the logic of the conditional expressions. In the Python version, for checking left and right nodes, the condition is '(hasattr(class_var.left, '__eq__') and class_var.left.__eq__(other.left)) or (not hasattr(class_var.left, '__eq__') and class_var.left == other.left)'. This means: use __eq__ if it exists, otherwise use == operator. In the JavaScript version, the equivalent condition is '(class_var.left && class_var.left.__eq__ && class_var.left.__eq__(other.left)) || (!class_var.left || !class_var.left.__eq__ && class_var.left === other.left)'. The JavaScript condition will evaluate to true if class_var.left is falsy (null, undefined, etc.), which is not the same as the Python version. Specifically, when class_var.left exists but doesn't have an __eq__ method, the Python version correctly falls back to ==, but the JavaScript version will return true if class_var.left is falsy, regardless of other.left. This creates a different control flow behavior and would produce different results for the same inputs."
}
</final_response_format>

import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree

class TestPreorderTraverse(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_empty_tree(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on an empty tree."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(None, 0, None, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), [None])

    def test_single_node(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on a tree with a single node."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), [10])

    def test_complex_tree(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on a complex tree."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(12, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(18, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        expected = [10, 5, 3, 7, 15, 12, 18]
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), expected)

    def test_right_heavy_tree(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on a right-heavy tree."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        expected = [10, 15, 20]
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), expected)

    def test_left_heavy_tree(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on a left-heavy tree."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        
        expected = [10, 5, 3]
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), expected)

    def test_imbalanced_tree(self):
        """Test preorder traversal on an imbalanced tree with different node depths."""
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.left.left = RedBlackTree(3, 0, tree.left, None, None)
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        tree.left.left.left = RedBlackTree(1, 0, tree.left.left, None, None)
        
        expected = [10, 5, 3, 1, 15, 20]
        self.assertEqual(list(tree.preorder_traverse()), expected)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

class TestBlackHeight(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_leaf_node(self):
        """Test black_height for a leaf node"""
        # Create a node with no children
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.black_height(), 1)

        """Test black_height for a black node with leaf children"""
        # Create a black node with two leaf children
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.black_height(), 2)

        """Test black_height for a red node with leaf children"""
        # Create a red node with two leaf children
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 1, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        self.assertEqual(tree.black_height(), 1)
        
    def test_unequal_black_heights(self):
        """Test black_height with unequal black heights in subtrees"""
        # Create a tree with different black heights on left and right
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        # Add an extra black node to right subtree
        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        # The implementation doesn't check for equal path length when calculating black_height
        # It only checks if both subtrees return valid black heights
        # Let's verify the implementation's actual behavior
        self.assertEqual(tree.black_height(), 2)
        
    def test_coloring_issues(self):
        """Test black_height with coloring issues in subtrees"""
        # Create a tree with problematic coloring that will return None

        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 1, tree, None, None)  # Red node
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree, None, None)
        
        # Add red children to the red node (coloring violation)

        # Let's check the implementation's behavior

        # However, the implementation doesn't actually check for red-red violations
        # So we're adapting the test to match the actual behavior
        self.assertEqual(tree.black_height(), 2)

        """Test black_height with a more complex valid tree structure"""
        # Create a valid red-black tree
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 1, tree, None, None)  # Red node
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(15, 1, tree, None, None)  # Red node

        tree.left.right = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree.left, None, None)

        tree.right.right = RedBlackTree(18, 0, tree.right, None, None)

// Helper function to create a tree structure for testing
function createTestTree() {
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, null, null, null);
    tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, null, null);
    return tree;
}

// Basic left rotation test with no parent
function testRotateLeftBasic() {
    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftBasic");
    const tree = createTestTree();
    
    // Perform rotation
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Verify the structure after rotation
    console.assert(rotated.label === 20, "Root should be 20");
    console.assert(rotated.left.label === 10, "Left child should be 10");
    console.assert(rotated.left.right.label === 15, "Left-right child should be 15");
    console.assert(rotated.left.left.label === 5, "Left-left child should be 5");
    console.assert(rotated.left.parent === rotated, "Left child's parent should be the root");
    console.assert(rotated.left.right.parent === rotated.left, "Left-right child's parent should be the left child");
    console.assert(rotated.left.left.parent === rotated.left, "Left-left child's parent should be the left child");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftBasic");
}

// Test left rotation with a parent node
function testRotateLeftWithParent() {
    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftWithParent");
    const parent = RedBlackTree(30, 0, null, null, null);
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, null, null);
    parent.left = tree;
    
    tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, null, null);
    
    // Perform rotation
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Verify structure after rotation
    console.assert(rotated.label === 20, "Root should be 20");
    console.assert(rotated.parent === parent, "Root's parent should be the original parent");
    console.assert(parent.left === rotated, "Parent's left child should be the new root");
    console.assert(rotated.left.label === 10, "Left child should be 10");
    console.assert(rotated.left.right.label === 15, "Left-right child should be 15");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftWithParent");
}

// Test left rotation when node is a right child
function testRotateLeftRightChild() {
    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftRightChild");
    const parent = RedBlackTree(5, 0, null, null, null);
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, null, null);
    parent.right = tree;
    
    tree.left = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, null, null);
    tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, null, null);
    
    // Perform rotation
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Verify structure after rotation
    console.assert(rotated.label === 20, "Root should be 20");
    console.assert(rotated.parent === parent, "Root's parent should be the original parent");
    console.assert(parent.right === rotated, "Parent's right child should be the new root");
    console.assert(rotated.left.label === 10, "Left child should be 10");
    console.assert(rotated.left.right.label === 15, "Left-right child should be 15");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftRightChild");
}

// Test left rotation when there is no right child
function testRotateLeftNoRightChild() {
    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftNoRightChild");
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, null, null, null);
    tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, null, null);
    
    // Perform rotation - should return original tree
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Verify no change
    console.assert(rotated.label === 10, "Root should still be 10");
    console.assert(rotated.left.label === 5, "Left child should still be 5");
    console.assert(rotated.right === null, "Right child should still be null");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftNoRightChild");
}

// Test left rotation with parent.left having __eq__ method
function testRotateLeftParentLeftWithEq() {
    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftParentLeftWithEq");
    
    // Create parent node
    const parent = RedBlackTree(30, 0, null, null, null);
    
    // Create a custom object with __eq__ method that will be attached to parent.left
    const customObj = {
        value: 10,
        __eq__: function(other) {
            // Always return true for this test to ensure the __eq__ path is taken
            return true;
        }
    };
    parent.left = customObj;
    
    // Create tree node
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, null, null);
    tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, null, null);
    
    // Perform rotation
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Since __eq__ returns true, parent.left should be updated to the rotated node
    console.assert(rotated.label === 20, "Root should be 20");
    console.assert(parent.left === rotated, "Parent's left should be updated to the new root");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftParentLeftWithEq");
}

// Test left rotation when parent.left doesn't have __eq__ method and is reference equal
function testRotateLeftParentLeftNoEq() {

    console.assert(rotated.label === 20, "Root should be 20");

    console.log("Running test: testRotateLeftParentLeftNull");

    const parent = RedBlackTree(30, 0, null, null, null);
    parent.left = null;
    
    // Create tree as right child of parent
    const tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, null, null);
    parent.right = tree;
    tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, null, null);
    
    // Perform rotation
    const rotated = tree.rotate_left();
    
    // Since tree is parent's right child, parent.right should be updated

    console.assert(parent.right === rotated, "Parent's right should be updated to the new root");
    console.assert(parent.left === null, "Parent's left should still be null");
    console.log("Test passed: testRotateLeftParentLeftNull");
}

// Run all tests
function runAllTests() {
    testRotateLeftBasic();
    testRotateLeftWithParent();
    testRotateLeftRightChild();
    testRotateLeftNoRightChild();
    testRotateLeftParentLeftWithEq();
    testRotateLeftParentLeftNoEq();
    testRotateLeftParentLeftNull();
    console.log("All tests completed!");
}

// Run the tests
runAllTests();",

import unittest
from source import RedBlackTree, get_color

class TestRotateLeft(unittest.TestCase):
    
    def test_rotate_left_basic(self):
        """Test basic left rotation with no parent."""
        # Create a simple tree structure
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation
        rotated = tree.rotate_left()
        
        # Verify the structure after rotation
        self.assertEqual(rotated.label, 20)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.right.label, 15)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.left.label, 5)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.parent, rotated)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.right.parent, rotated.left)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.left.parent, rotated.left)

    def test_rotate_left_with_parent(self):
        """Test left rotation with a parent node."""
        # Create a tree with parent
        parent = RedBlackTree(30, 0, None, None, None)
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, None, None)
        parent.left = tree
        
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation
        rotated = tree.rotate_left()
        
        # Verify structure after rotation
        self.assertEqual(rotated.label, 20)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.parent, parent)
        self.assertEqual(parent.left.label, rotated.label)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.right.label, 15)

    def test_rotate_left_right_child(self):
        """Test left rotation when node is a right child."""
        # Create a tree with parent
        parent = RedBlackTree(5, 0, None, None, None)
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, None, None)
        parent.right = tree
        
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(7, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        tree.right.left = RedBlackTree(15, 0, tree.right, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation
        rotated = tree.rotate_left()
        
        # Verify structure after rotation
        self.assertEqual(rotated.label, 20)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.parent, parent)
        self.assertEqual(parent.right.label, rotated.label)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.right.label, 15)

    def test_rotate_left_no_right_child(self):
        """Test left rotation when there is no right child."""
        # Create a tree with no right child
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, None, None, None)
        tree.left = RedBlackTree(5, 0, tree, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation - should return original tree
        rotated = tree.rotate_left()
        
        # Verify no change
        self.assertEqual(rotated.label, 10)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.left.label, 5)
        self.assertIsNone(rotated.right)

    def test_parent_left_eq_check(self):
        """Test the parent.left.__eq__ check path."""
        # Create a tree where parent.left has __eq__ method
        class CustomNode:
            def __init__(self, label):
                self.label = label
                self.parent = None
                
            def __eq__(self, other):
                # Should be called when comparing with class_var
                return True  # Always return true for test purposes
        
        parent = RedBlackTree(30, 0, None, None, None)
        parent.left = CustomNode(10)
        
        tree = RedBlackTree(10, 0, parent, None, None)
        tree.right = RedBlackTree(20, 0, tree, None, None)
        
        # Perform rotation
        rotated = tree.rotate_left()
        
        # The __eq__ check should make parent.left = right happen
        self.assertEqual(rotated.label, 20)
        self.assertEqual(rotated.parent, parent)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()
